message:
fix(frankenterm-core): update scroll caller sites for BCE bg param + fix scroll fixture hex (bd-39lcx)

Update all test harnesses (conformance, differential, model-check,
proptest, bench) to pass cursor bg color to scroll_up/scroll_down/
scroll_up_into/scroll_down_from/insert_lines/delete_lines after the
BCE signature change in 013e27d0.

Add 8 scroll-with-bg-color conformance fixtures (SU, SD, IL, DL, IND,
RI, newline scroll) with corrected fill hex (5×3 = 15 chars, not 16).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-397","title":"Create adversarial tests for escape injection","description":"TESTING - ADVERSARIAL SECURITY TESTS

## Background
FrankenTUI must be secure against untrusted content. Escape sequences in user-provided text could corrupt the terminal or execute malicious sequences. This implements ADR-006 (Sanitize-by-Default).

## Threat Model

### Attack Vectors
1. **Log injection**: Untrusted output contains escape sequences
2. **Title injection**: OSC sequences to change window title
3. **Clipboard injection**: OSC 52 to set clipboard
4. **Cursor corruption**: CSI sequences to move cursor
5. **Mode hijacking**: Enabling raw mode, mouse, etc.

### Example Malicious Inputs
- "\x1b[2J" - Clear screen
- "\x1b[H" - Home cursor
- "\x1b]0;pwned\x07" - Set window title
- "\x1b]52;c;SGVsbG8=\x07" - Set clipboard
- "\x1b[?1049h" - Enter alt screen
- "\x1b[?1000h" - Enable mouse
- "\x1b[31mred\x1b[0m" - Color injection

## Technical Requirements

### Sanitizer Implementation
pub fn sanitize_for_display(input: &str) -> Cow<str> {
    // Fast path: no control characters
    if !input.bytes().any(|b| b < 0x20 || b == 0x7F || b == 0x1B) {
        return Cow::Borrowed(input);
    }
    
    // Slow path: filter control sequences
    let mut output = String::with_capacity(input.len());
    let mut chars = input.chars().peekable();
    
    while let Some(c) = chars.next() {
        match c {
            '\x1b' => skip_escape_sequence(&mut chars),
            c if c.is_control() && c != '\n' && c != '\t' => {
                // Replace with visual representation
                output.push_str(&format!("^{}", (c as u8 + 64) as char));
            }
            _ => output.push(c),
        }
    }
    
    Cow::Owned(output)
}

### Test Categories

#### 1. Escape Sequence Filtering
- [ ] ESC [ ... (CSI) filtered
- [ ] ESC ] ... (OSC) filtered
- [ ] ESC P ... (DCS) filtered
- [ ] ESC _ ... (APC) filtered
- [ ] ESC ^ ... (PM) filtered

#### 2. Control Character Handling
- [ ] NUL (0x00) filtered
- [ ] BEL (0x07) filtered
- [ ] BS (0x08) filtered
- [ ] Tab (0x09) preserved
- [ ] LF (0x0A) preserved
- [ ] CR (0x0D) handled
- [ ] DEL (0x7F) filtered

#### 3. Incomplete Sequence Handling
- [ ] Truncated ESC at end
- [ ] Truncated CSI at end
- [ ] Truncated OSC at end
- [ ] Invalid sequence bytes

#### 4. Raw Passthrough (Explicit Opt-In)
- [ ] raw_text() API exists
- [ ] Loud documentation warning
- [ ] Works for ANSI colors when intended

## PTY Tests

### Test Script
for payload in "${MALICIOUS_PAYLOADS[@]}"; do
    # Run payload through ftui log viewer
    echo "$payload" | ftui-test-harness
    
    # Verify terminal state unchanged
    verify_terminal_state
    
    # Verify no escape sequences in output
    verify_no_escapes
done

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All ESC/CSI/OSC/DCS/APC filtered by default
- [ ] Control characters filtered except tab/newline
- [ ] Fast path for clean input (no allocation)
- [ ] Raw passthrough API documented with warnings
- [ ] PTY tests verify terminal state preserved
- [ ] Fuzz testing for parser robustness

 {"id":"bd-39vx","title":"Modal/Dialog System with Proper Stacking","description":"## Background

FrankenTUI needs modals for:
- Confirmations ('Delete file?')
- Prompts ('Enter filename:')
- Complex dialogs (settings, wizards)
- Help overlay (already exists, but ad-hoc)
- Command palette (planned in bd-39y4)

Current approach: Each feature implements its own overlay logic.

## Solution: Modal Layer System

```rust
/// Modal size presets
#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy)]
pub enum ModalSize {
    Small,      // 40x10
    Medium,     // 60x20
    Large,      // 80%x80%
    FullScreen,
    Custom { width: Constraint, height: Constraint },
}

/// A modal dialog
pub struct Modal {
    pub id: ModalId,
    pub content: Box<dyn Widget>,
    pub size: ModalSize,
    pub title: Option<String>,
    pub dismissable: bool,  // Escape closes
    pub backdrop: BackdropStyle,
    pub animation: Option<ModalAnimation>,
    pub on_dismiss: Option<Box<dyn FnOnce() + Send>>,
}

/// Backdrop behind modal
#[derive(Debug, Clone)]
pub enum BackdropStyle {
    None,
    Dim { opacity: f32 },  // 0.0-1.0
    Blur,  // If terminal supports
    Custom(Style),
}

/// Modal animation
#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy)]
pub enum ModalAnimation {
    FadeIn { duration: Duration },
    SlideUp { duration: Duration },
    Scale { duration: Duration },
}

/// Manages modal stack
pub struct ModalLayer {
    stack: Vec<Modal>,
    focus_manager: FocusManager,  // Uses #2
}

impl ModalLayer {
    pub fn push(&mut self, modal: Modal) -> ModalId;
    pub fn pop(&mut self) -> Option<Modal>;
    pub fn close(&mut self, id: ModalId);
    pub fn is_open(&self, id: ModalId) -> bool;
    pub fn has_modals(&self) -> bool;
    pub fn render(&self, frame: &mut Frame); // Renders all modals
}

// Convenience builders
pub fn confirm(title: &str, message: &str) -> ConfirmDialogBuilder;
pub fn prompt(title: &str, placeholder: &str) -> PromptDialogBuilder;
pub fn alert(title: &str, message: &str) -> AlertDialogBuilder;

// Usage
let result = confirm("Delete?", "This cannot be undone")
    .with_cancel("Cancel")
    .with_confirm("Delete")
    .danger()  // Red confirm button
    .show(&mut modal_layer);
```

## Visual Design

```
┌──────────── Delete File? ────────────┐
│                                       │
│  Are you sure you want to delete     │
│  'important.txt'?                    │
│                                       │
│  This action cannot be undone.       │
│                                       │
│           [Cancel]  [Delete]          │
└───────────────────────────────────────┘
```

## Focus Trapping (uses #2)

- Modal pushes focus trap on open
- Tab cycles within modal
- Escape pops modal and restores focus

## Files to Create

- crates/ftui-widgets/src/modal/mod.rs
- crates/ftui-widgets/src/modal/confirm.rs
- crates/ftui-widgets/src/modal/prompt.rs
- crates/ftui-widgets/src/modal/alert.rs
- crates/ftui-runtime/src/modal_layer.rs

## Success Criteria
- Epic delivers a cohesive user-visible capability: Modal/Dialog System with Proper Stacking
- Child tasks are not just implemented; each has unit tests + E2E scripts with verbose JSONL logs and deterministic fixtures
- Graceful degradation tiers are specified (capabilities, fallbacks, safety) and validated in tests
- Formal invariants documented + enforced via property tests; any dynamic decisions have an evidence ledger and debug UI


## Alien Artifact Upgrades
- Model the core decision(s) with explicit state, priors/assumptions, and invariants; write down failure modes as first-class artifacts.
- Explainability: add an evidence ledger for any non-trivial decision (why this branch, why this fallback). Prefer Bayes factors / log-likelihood contributions over ad-hoc scoring.
- Guarantees: where prediction/thresholding exists, prefer distribution-free calibration (conformal prediction) or anytime-valid testing (e-process) so we can stop early without invalid inference.
- Graceful degradation: define capability tiers + deterministic fallbacks; never fail closed without a traceable reason.


## Extreme Optimization
- Baseline first: capture p50/p95/p99 for relevant command(s) with `hyperfine` (store raw output).
- Profile next: CPU flamegraph + allocation profile; identify top-5 hotspots.
- Opportunity matrix: implement only Score ≥ 2.0 (Impact×Confidence/Effort).
- Isomorphism proof per change: ordering, tie-breaking, RNG seeds, and golden checksums must match.
- Regression gates: add microbench + budgets; failures must print actionable deltas and include log artifacts.


## Testing Plan
- Unit tests: happy path + edge cases + regression fixtures.
- Property tests: invariants (idempotence, monotonicity, determinism) where applicable.
- Fuzz tests: parsers/state machines and any input-facing logic.
- Determinism: fixed seeds/time-freeze; snapshot fixtures must be stable.
- Metamorphic tests: shrink/grow, reorder non-semantic inputs, and ensure invariants still hold.

 {"id":"bd-39vx.1","title":"Modal Container Widget","description":"Core modal widget that renders content in overlay layer.

## Requirements
- Centered positioning with configurable offset
- Backdrop with configurable opacity
- Size constraints (min/max width/height)
- Close on backdrop click (optional)

## Proposed API

```rust
pub struct Modal<C: Widget> {
    content: C,
    config: ModalConfig,
    state: ModalState,
}

#[derive(Clone)]
pub struct ModalConfig {
    pub position: ModalPosition,
    pub backdrop: BackdropConfig,
    pub size: SizeConstraints,
    pub close_on_backdrop: bool,
    pub close_on_escape: bool,
}

#[derive(Clone)]
pub enum ModalPosition {
    Center,
    CenterOffset { x: i16, y: i16 },
    TopCenter { margin: u16 },
    Custom { x: u16, y: u16 },
}

#[derive(Clone)]
pub struct BackdropConfig {
    pub color: Color,
    pub opacity: f32,  // 0.0 - 1.0
}

#[derive(Clone)]
pub struct SizeConstraints {
    pub min_width: Option<u16>,
    pub max_width: Option<u16>,
    pub min_height: Option<u16>,
    pub max_height: Option<u16>,
}

pub enum ModalMsg {
    Close,
    BackdropClicked,
    EscapePressed,
}
```

## Rendering Strategy
1. Render backdrop to full screen area
2. Calculate content bounds from constraints
3. Center content within available space
4. Render content on top of backdrop
5. Use overlay layer (z-index management)

## File: crates/ftui-widgets/src/modal/container.rs

## Unit Tests
- test_center_calculation: Various screen sizes
- test_offset_positioning: Offset from center
- test_size_constraints: Min/max enforcement
- test_backdrop_click: Click detection on backdrop vs content

## Snapshot Tests
- modal_center_80x24, modal_offset_80x24
- modal_constrained_120x40, modal_backdrop_opacity

## Acceptance Criteria
- Deliver the behavior implied by the title: Modal Container Widget
- Alien Artifact: define invariants + failure modes; if heuristics exist, specify an explainable decision rule (evidence ledger / Bayes factors where applicable)
- Extreme Optimization: baseline -> profile -> opportunity matrix; for each perf change include an isomorphism proof + golden output checksums
- Unit tests: happy path + edge cases + regression fixtures; add property tests for core invariants where applicable
- E2E: scripts with verbose JSONL logging (env, capabilities, timings, seed, checksums) + deterministic mode

 {"id":"bd-39vx.2","title":"Dialog Presets","description":"Common dialog patterns built on Modal.

## Requirements
- Alert dialog (message + OK button)
- Confirm dialog (message + OK/Cancel)
- Prompt dialog (message + input + OK/Cancel)
- Custom dialog builder

## Implementation
- Dialog enum with Alert, Confirm, Prompt variants
- DialogBuilder for custom dialogs
- Async result handling via Cmd

## Tests
- Each dialog type renders correctly
- Button actions work
- Input capture in prompt

## Acceptance Criteria
- Deliver the behavior implied by the title: Dialog Presets
- Alien Artifact: define invariants + failure modes; if heuristics exist, specify an explainable decision rule (evidence ledger / Bayes factors where applicable)
- Extreme Optimization: baseline -> profile -> opportunity matrix; for each perf change include an isomorphism proof + golden output checksums
- Unit tests: happy path + edge cases + regression fixtures; add property tests for core invariants where applicable
- E2E: scripts with verbose JSONL logging (env, capabilities, timings, seed, checksums) + deterministic mode

 {"id":"bd-lff4p.2.5","title":"Implement: WebGPU text rendering (atlas + instanced quads)","description":"Goal
- Render terminal text cells using the glyph atlas and an instanced quad pipeline.

Requirements
- Respect per-cell fg color + attrs (bold/italic/underline as feasible).
- Handle wide glyphs and combining marks (at least gracefully).

Acceptance Criteria
- 120x40 grid @ 60fps with typical demo workloads on modern desktop.
- Instrumented frame timing (CPU submit + GPU time if possible).
","notes":"2026-02-08 IndigoRaven: wired true glyph-atlas texture sampling path in frankenterm-web renderer. Added atlas texture/sampler + glyph metadata storage buffer bindings in WGSL/pipeline; patch application now resolves glyph ids into atlas slots and uploads atlas/metadata on demand; renderer now samples atlas alpha per cell for fg ink (with underline/strikethrough attrs). Added public atlas pixel exposure in glyph_atlas for GPU upload bridge. Current glyph raster source is deterministic procedural fallback; awaiting bd-lff4p.2.4 final raster/metadata contract to swap in production font rasterization.

2026-02-08 IndigoRaven: follow-up polish: batch atlas upload once per apply_patches (avoid N× full-atlas uploads when many new glyphs appear in one frame), treat whitespace as glyph_id=0 (no atlas slot), and key glyph slot mapping by GlyphKey (codepoint+px_size) with redundant glyph_meta writes avoided via CPU-side equality check.

2026-02-08 IndigoRaven: round cell pixel sizes in WebGpuRenderer init/resize so viewport+cell metrics stay aligned on fractional DPR (avoids subtle drift/stretched quads under zoom).

2026-02-09 AzureFox: completed non-overlapping instrumentation/determinism slice while renderer/glyph work proceeds in parallel. Added optional per-frame CPU-submit/GPU timing support + optional histograms and robust JSONL serialization in crates/frankenterm-web/src/frame_harness.rs; added patch_batch_stats helper + tests in crates/frankenterm-web/src/patch_feed.rs; updated crates/frankenterm-web/benches/renderer_bench.rs to consume patch_batch_stats and populate new FrameRecord timing fields.  {"id":"bd-lff4p.2.6","title":"Implement: cursor + selection + hyperlink hover/click rendering","description":"Goal
- Make the terminal feel interactive: cursor, selection visualization, and hyperlink affordances.

Requirements
- Cursor rendering styles: block, bar, underline.
- Selection rendering + copy.
- Hyperlink hover underline + click dispatch.

Acceptance Criteria
- Deterministic visual tests (golden images or checksumable framebuffer dumps).
","notes":"2026-02-09 AzureFox: implemented interaction pipeline slice for cursor/selection/hyperlink in frankenterm-web. patch_feed now packs attrs as style bits (low 8) plus link_id (high 24) with tests including cell_from_render_packs_style_and_link_id. renderer now decodes packed attrs, adds interaction uniforms for hovered-link/cursor/selection overlays, and shader rendering for hyperlink-hover underline plus cursor styles (block/bar/underline) plus selection inversion; added tests for packed attr decode, cursor style mapping, and uniform layout. wasm API extended with setCursor, setSelectionRange, clearSelection, setHoveredLinkId, linkAt, drainLinkClicks, extractSelectionText; mouse input now updates hover and queues link click dispatch events; resize/destroy synchronize and clear interaction state.  {"id":"bd-lff4p.2.7","title":"Implement: web input capture layer (keyboard/mouse/touch)","description":"Goal
- Capture browser input events and feed them into the ftui/FrankenTerm world with minimal latency and deterministic semantics.

Requirements
- Keyboard
  - consistent modifier semantics across browsers/OS
  - repeat handling
  - special keys and function keys
  - composition events integrate with IME plan (bd-lff4p.2.9)
- Mouse
  - click/drag/wheel with correct cell coordinates (respects DPR + font metrics)
  - selection gestures
- Touch
  - tap, long-press, scroll; optional pinch zoom

Testing + Logging
- Unit tests for key mapping tables and edge cases (Ctrl/Alt/Meta/Super).
- Property tests for modifier state machines (no stuck modifiers; idempotent focus loss).
- E2E scripts emit JSONL: event counts, encoded bytes (when relevant), and any mapping fallbacks.

Acceptance Criteria
- Deterministic event encoding suitable for record/replay.
- No key ghosting; no stuck modifiers; focus changes handled correctly.
","notes":"NavyCanyon is actively implementing frankenterm-web input schema/module + tests; asked others to avoid overlapping edits in crates/frankenterm-web until push (see Agent Mail thread bd-lff4p.2.7).","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"GoldForest","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:52:19.592183195Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-08T19:56:54.271201447Z","closed_at":"2026-02-08T19:56:54.271180678Z","close_reason":"Implemented input schema + wasm glue (key/mouse/wheel/touch/composition/focus) with stable JSON encoding + tests; wasm32 check passes","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.2.7","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:52:19.592183195Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.2.7","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.2.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:52:19.592183195Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.2.7","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:52:19.592183195Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

Acceptance Criteria

- Works across Chrome/Safari/Firefox within their security constraints.

+{"id":"bd-lff4p.2.8","title":"Implement: clipboard integration (copy/paste) for frankenterm-web","description":"Goal
- Implement copy and paste behaviors expected from a modern terminal.

Acceptance Criteria
- Copy uses selection model; paste injects as input stream.
- Works across Chrome/Safari/Firefox within their security constraints.
","notes":"2026-02-09 ChartreusePond progress: implemented explicit clipboard integration surface in frankenterm-web. Added normalized InputEvent::Paste/PasteInput path with stable JSON roundtrip (kind="paste") and VT encoding via encode_paste_text (respects bracketedPaste). Added wasm APIs: pasteText(text) to inject clipboard payload into encoded input stream and copySelection() to extract current selection text for host-managed clipboard writes. Added payload guard MAX_PASTE_BYTES=786432 (protocol-aligned). parse_input_event now accepts kind="paste". Updated docs/getting-started.md with browser-safe flow (user-gesture clipboard read/write in host JS; Chrome/Safari/Firefox constraints). Validation: cargo test -p frankenterm-web (101+21 tests pass), cargo check -p frankenterm-web, cargo check -p frankenterm-web --target wasm32-unknown-unknown, cargo check --all-targets, cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings. cargo fmt --check currently fails due unrelated concurrent edits in frankenterm-core/ftui-extras files not in this bead.","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"ChartreusePond","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:52:24.704918895Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-09T05:47:41.031270667Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.2.8","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:52:24.704918895Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.2.8","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.2.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:52:24.704918895Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.2.8","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.2.7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:52:24.704918895Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-lff4p.2.9","title":"Implement: IME/composition support (CJK input)","description":"Goal
- Provide high-quality text input beyond raw key events.

Acceptance Criteria
- Composition events do not corrupt terminal state.
- Works with common IMEs (macOS, Windows, Linux).
","notes":"Implemented IME composition normalization in frankenterm-web: added CompositionState/CompositionRewrite to canonicalize start/update/end/cancel streams, suppress key events during active composition, synthesize missing start events for malformed host sequences, and emit synthetic cancel on focus-loss to prevent stuck composition state. Wired wasm input ingestion through CompositionState rewrite path. Expanded composition phase parsing aliases (compositionstart/update/end/cancel + commit alias), and made composition data parsing strict-optional (type-checked when present). Added unit tests: composition_update_without_start_synthesizes_start, composition_drops_key_events_until_end, composition_focus_loss_emits_cancel_before_focus_event. Validation: cargo test -p frankenterm-web (pass), cargo check -p frankenterm-web --all-targets (pass), cargo clippy -p frankenterm-web --all-targets -- -D warnings (pass), cargo check -p frankenterm-web --target wasm32-unknown-unknown (pass). Workspace-wide gates currently fail due unrelated concurrent changes in ftui-harness/ftui-demo-showcase/ftui-runtime.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"BrightDune","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:52:29.229709842Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-08T20:22:13.809457202Z","closed_at":"2026-02-08T20:22:13.809432977Z","close_reason":"Completed: IME composition normalization + tests + wasm compile validation","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.2.9","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:52:29.229709842Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.2.9","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.2.7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:52:29.229709842Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-lff4p.3","title":"ftui-web: compile ftui to WASM and run inside FrankenTerm","description":"Scope
- Compile ftui (core/render/layout/style/text/widgets/runtime + demo) to wasm and run inside FrankenTerm surface.
- Replace terminal polling with a step-based, JS-driven event loop.

Deliverables
- New `ftui-web` crate exposing a `WasmApp` API: new, input, resize, tick, render.
- Minimal web harness that runs ftui-demo-showcase fully client-side.
","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:49:24.446759480Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-08T08:49:24.446759480Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.3","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:49:24.446759480Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-lff4p.3.1","title":"Implement: new crate ftui-web (WASM API surface + glue)","description":"Goal
- Add `crates/ftui-web` exposing a minimal embedding API for running ftui models in WASM.

API Sketch
- `WasmApp::new(opts)`
- `resize(cols, rows)`
- `input(event_bytes or semantic event)`
- `tick(dt)`
- `render() -> Patch or Frame`

Acceptance Criteria
- `cargo check -p ftui-web --target wasm32-unknown-unknown` succeeds.
","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:52:57.316110858Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-08T19:53:07.043612601Z","closed_at":"2026-02-08T19:53:07.043593735Z","close_reason":"Implemented ftui-web crate scaffold; wasm check passes (d224e106)","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.3.1","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:52:57.316110858Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.3.1","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:52:57.316110858Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.3.1","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.9","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:52:57.316110858Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
